description: replace cmplt+mask_add with vpminud in mul/dot_product reductions — no effect on AMD Zen 3
fuse last two DFT layers in second_half — regression, likely losing backwards cache locality optimization
remove confuse_compiler from mul — LLVM emits vpmullq, slight regression confirms barrier is needed
manual unroll-by-4 in DitButterfly inner loop — massive regression, broke compiler vectorization
upgrade inline to inline(always) on mul and helpers — slight regression from I-cache pressure
fuse last two layers in second_half_general (v2, proper backwards) — still worse than alternation

iter: NOTE: Iters 1-6 ran WITHOUT target-cpu=native (SSE2 only, 2.65s baseline). Baseline re-saved with AVX2 (1.41s). Iters 7+ use AVX2.

description: inline(always) on apply_to_rows — no effect, compiler already inlines with AVX2
fuse last two layers in second_half_general with AVX2 — no effect
unsafe pointer ops in DitButterfly to skip bounds checks — no effect, compiler already elides
shift mid split point +1 (11 instead of 10) — regression, balanced split is already optimal
shift mid split point -1 (9 instead of 10) — test failures for small log_h values
specialized apply_to_rows for TwiddleFreeButterfly — no effect, compiler already inlines chain

iter: SUMMARY: After 12 iterations, no improvements found. Key blocker: AVX2 arithmetic code is read-only. DFT algorithm is well-tuned (backwards alt, twiddle-free, pre-broadcast, scaled butterfly). Best opportunity was enabling target-cpu=native (47% speedup from AVX2, build config not code change).
